svg.text: Probabilistic data structure for O(1) membership testing with controlled false positive rates
Query: Check bits 4, 11, 19 - If ALL set, probably exists. If ANY unset, definitely not present.
Total: 400K keys | Memory: 400KB | FP: ~1% per filter
Memory vs False Positive Rate Trade-Offs
FP Rate ≈ (1 - e^(-kn/m))^k | Optimal k = (m/n) * ln(2)
Larger bit arrays reduce false positives but increase memory. Find the sweet spot
based on acceptable FP rate for your use case. Start with 1% FP as baseline.
